text: As artificial intelligence becomes more common in our daily lives, important ethical questions arise. One major concern is bias: if an AI system is trained on unfair data, it can make biased decisions about jobs, loans, or even medical care. This makes fairness essential—developers must ensure AI treats all groups equally. Privacy is another key issue, since AI often collects and analyzes personal information. People have the right to know how their data is used. Transparency also matters; users should understand how AI reaches its conclusions. Ultimately, responsible AI development means building systems that are ethical, safe, and respectful of human rights, with clear accountability for their actions.
